briefdescription.para: Enumerate type for debug and log.

detaileddescription.para: @Synopsis MOOSE's random device. Use it from <random>
@Synopsis Global random number generator engine. Everywhere we use this engine.

briefdescription.para: A global seed for all RNGs in moose. When moose.seed( x ) is called, this variable is set. Other's RNGs (except muparser) uses this seed to initialize them. By default it is initialized by random_device (see global.cpp).

detaileddescription.para: List of characters considered to be whitespace
@Synopsis Global RNG.
@Synopsis Add solver performance into the global map.
@Param name Name of the solver. @Param time Time taken by the solver. @Param steps Steps.

briefdescription.para: Givem path of MOOSE element, return its name. It's behaviour is like basename of unix command e.g. /a/b/c --> c.
Checks if given path is correct. If not, return false and error-code as well.

simplesect.para: 0 if path is all-right. Negative number if path is not OK.

briefdescription.para: Create a POSIX compatible path from a given string. Remove/replace bad characters.

parameterdescription.para: Reutrn path is given path if creation was successful, else directory is renamed to a filename.

briefdescription.para: Create directories recursively needed to open the given file p.

detaileddescription.para: Create directory, recursively.

parameterdescription.para: When successfully created, returns created path, else convert path to a filename by replacing '/' by '_'.

memberdef.argsstring: (string msg, string prefix="DEBUG", string color=T_RESET, unsigned debugLevel=0)
memberdef.name: debugPrint

detaileddescription.para: Fix the user-given path whenever possible

briefdescription.para: Fix a path. For testing purpose.

detaileddescription.para: @Synopsis Get environment valus.
@Param env Name of the environment variable.
@Returns value of environment if set, empty string otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get the extension of a given filepath.

detaileddescription.para: @Synopsis Get the global seed set by call of moose.seed( X )
@Returns seed (int).

briefdescription.para: Append pathB to pathA and return the result.

detaileddescription.para: If pathA does not have [indexs] at the end, append "[0]" to pathA and then add pathB to it. This version does not care if the result has '[0]' at its end.

memberdef.argsstring: (string msg, serverity_level_ type=debug, bool redirectToConsole=true, bool removeTicks=true)
memberdef.name: log

briefdescription.para: Log to console (and to a log-file)

detaileddescription.para: @Synopsis Generate a suitable column name based on given path. Replace [\d+] with \d+; and remove [\0+]. Replace / with given delim (default '.')
@Param path (string). @Param delim (char) @Param maxLevel (int). Max number of parents to include. Default 1.
@Returns Reformatted path suitable for column name.

sect2.title: Example:
sect2.para: @Synopsis Genearate a suitable string for column name which can be used in a spreadsheet/numpy table/csv file etc.

briefdescription.para: When user gives a path /a/b/c, moose creates a path /a[0]/b[0]/c[0]. This is helpful in cases where one needs to create more than 1 element.

detaileddescription.para: @Synopsis Overloaded function. Random number between a and b
@Param a lower limit. @Param b Upper limit.
@Returns

briefdescription.para: Generate a random double between 0 and 1.

detaileddescription.para: Set the global seed or all rngs.
@Synopsis Normalize given path.
@Param path
@Returns
@Synopsis Normalize a given path by removing multiple repeating // to / and /./ to /
@Param path
@Returns

briefdescription.para: Return the name when path is given. Its behaviour is exactly the same as of basename command on unix system.

detaileddescription.para: Generate random string of given length.
@Synopsis Set the seed for all random generator. When seed of a RNG is not set, this seed it used. It is set to -1 by default.
@Param seed

memberdef.argsstring: (size_t max, size_t n, std::vector< std::pair< size_t, size_t > > &result)
memberdef.name: splitIntervalInNParts

detaileddescription.para: @Synopsis Split a interval (0, max) in n parts which are almost equal to each other.
@Param max @Param n @Param result. A vector of interval [start, end) (as std::pair).
@Synopsis Split a given path into (parent, name). The behaviour of this function is akin to unix'; dirname and basepath.
@Param path
@Returns A pair of strings <parent, name>

briefdescription.para: Compares the two strings a and b for first n characters, ignoring the case of the characters. Return 0 in case both are same upto first n characters. Othere a non-zero value is returned. When n is smaller or equal to the size of both strings, positive is return if a is larger than b, or negative when a is smaller than b.

detaileddescription.para: When n is larger than size of a or b, non-zero values is returned when a and b are not equal upto min(n, min(a.size(), b.size())) characters.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::string &text, const std::string &prefix="", const size_t width=70)
memberdef.name: textwrap

briefdescription.para: Replace all directory sepearator with _. This creates a filepath which can be created in current directory without any need to create parent directory.

detaileddescription.para: Splits given string into tokens

briefdescription.para: Convert a given value to string.

detaileddescription.para: trims the leading and trailing white spaces
@Synopsis Converts a vector to string.
@Param vec
@Returns
The CompartmentBase class sets up the interface for all the derived Compartment classes, used in branched nerve calculations. Handles electronic structure and also channels.

The Compartment class sets up an asymmetric compartment for branched nerve calculations. Handles electronic structure and also channels. This is not a particularly efficient way of doing this, so we should use a solver for any substantial calculations.
